acceptance_criteria: Given a registered user visits the login page, when they enter valid credentials and click Sign In, then they are redirected to the dashboard with a personalized welcome message.
Given a user enters an incorrect password, when they click Sign In, then they see an error message 'Username and password do not match' and remain on the login page.
Given a user leaves username or password blank, when they click Sign In, then field-level validation errors are shown.
Given a locked-out account, when the user attempts to log in, then a 'Your account has been locked' message is shown.
Given an MFA-enabled account, after valid credentials, the user is prompted for a 6-digit TOTP code before accessing the dashboard.
Given a logged-in user, when they click logout, then the session is cleared and they are redirected to the login page.

summary: User Management REST API – CRUD Operations
acceptance_criteria: Given a POST /api/users request with valid name and job fields, the response status is 201 and the body contains an auto-generated id and createdAt timestamp.
Given a GET /api/users/{id} request for an existing user, the response status is 200 and the body contains the user's data object.
Given a GET /api/users/{id} request for a non-existent user id, the response status is 404.
Given a PUT /api/users/{id} request with a full user payload, the response status is 200 and the body contains an updatedAt timestamp.
Given a PATCH /api/users/{id} request with partial fields, the response status is 200 and only the specified fields are updated.
Given a DELETE /api/users/{id} request, the response status is 204 and the user no longer exists.
Given a GET /api/users?page=2 request, the response returns users for the second page with a correct page field in the body.
Given a logged-in user on the dashboard, all widget data loads within 3 seconds.
Given the dashboard is loaded, each widget displays a loading skeleton before data arrives.
Given the user has no data for a widget, a friendly empty-state illustration with a call-to-action is shown.
Given the user refreshes the dashboard, all widget data is refreshed without a full page reload.
Given a data fetch error occurs, a retry button is displayed on the affected widget.
